A perfect sourdough bread is characterized by its high-quality, airy crumb and a thin, crispy crust. Here are five actionable ways you can improve your technique to achieve professional-grade results:

### 1. Master the Hydration and Cold Fermentation
For a professional-grade sourdough bread, you must focus on the hydration level of your dough. Aim for a hydration of between 75% and 80%, which creates an open, airy crumb. To manage this sticky dough, use a cold fermentation process by letting the dough proof in the refrigerator for twelve to twenty-four hours. This allows the wild yeast to develop complex flavor profiles and creates a light, bubbly structure that is difficult to achieve with quick-rise recipes.
